title: Project Foundation & MCP Hub Core
description: Scaffold the TypeScript project, set up the MCP server with Streamable HTTP transport, implement the MCP tool aggregator/proxy that connects to downstream MCPs via stdio, and build the core daemon infrastructure (start/stop/status CLI, PID management, SQLite setup). By end of this feature the SERVICE daemon starts, exposes a valid MCP server at http://localhost:3333/mcp, and an AI agent can connect to it and call tools from a downstream test MCP.

tasks: Initialize package.json with TypeScript, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, better-sqlite3, commander, express, chalk, ora, tsx
Set up tsconfig.json with strict mode, ESM output, Node.js types
Create src/index.ts — main daemon entry point
Create src/server/mcp-hub.ts — the core MCP server using McpServer from SDK with StreamableHTTPServerTransport on port 3333
Create src/server/proxy.ts — spawns downstream MCP stdio servers as child processes, forwards tool calls, aggregates tool lists
Create src/db/database.ts — SQLite schema: integrations, notifications, agent_sessions, mcp_connections tables
Create src/cli/index.ts — CLI entry using commander: 'service start', 'service stop', 'service status' commands
Create src/daemon/manager.ts — daemonize process, write PID file to ~/.service/service.pid
Write unit tests for tool aggregation and proxy routing
Create bin/service script that runs the CLI
npm scripts: build, start, test, service (runs CLI)
technicalNotes: Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk Server class with StreamableHTTPServerTransport (express + POST /mcp endpoint)
For downstream MCP stdio: spawn child process, pipe stdio, implement JSON-RPC 2.0 multiplexing
Tool namespacing: prefix each downstream tool with its MCP server ID to avoid collisions, e.g. 'github__create_issue'
SQLite better-sqlite3 (synchronous) is fine for this use case
PID file at ~/.service/service.pid, logs at ~/.service/logs/
Config at ~/.service/config.json
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build succeeds with no TypeScript errors
npm test passes all unit tests
node dist/cli/index.js start launches daemon and writes PID file
curl http://localhost:3333/mcp returns valid MCP response
node dist/cli/index.js status shows running state
node dist/cli/index.js stop stops the daemon cleanly

title: Notification Gateway & Push System
description: Build the 24/7 notification gateway that listens for events from connected integrations and pushes them to AI agents in real time. Implement the IntegrationAdapter interface, the notification storage layer, and the SSE push mechanism so agents always have up-to-date context about what's happening across platforms. Include the base integration framework and a built-in 'echo' test integration.

tasks: Create src/integrations/types.ts — IntegrationAdapter interface, NotificationEvent type, IntegrationConfig type with JSONSchema for config fields
Create src/integrations/registry.ts — IntegrationRegistry class: load/unload adapters, store configs in SQLite, hot-reload
Create src/gateway/notification-store.ts — persist all incoming events to notifications table with timestamp, source, payload, read status
Create src/gateway/push-manager.ts — manages SSE connections from agents, broadcasts new notifications, supports per-agent subscription filters
Extend MCP hub: add 'service__get_notifications', 'service__subscribe_notifications', 'service__mark_read' tools
Create src/integrations/builtin/echo.ts — test integration that generates synthetic events on a timer
Create src/integrations/builtin/webhook.ts — generic webhook integration: registers an HTTP endpoint, any POST to it creates a notification
Update database schema: notifications table with id, integration_id, event_type, payload JSON, created_at, read_at, agent_id
Write tests: notification storage, push delivery, SSE connection lifecycle
technicalNotes: SSE endpoint at GET /events on port 3334 (admin API) — agents connect here for real-time stream
Also expose notifications as MCP tool results: 'service__get_notifications' returns recent unread events
Use Node.js EventEmitter as internal bus between gateway and push manager
Each notification: { id, source: 'integration_id', type: 'event_type', title, body, metadata, timestamp }
Agents can call 'service__subscribe_notifications' to register interest in specific sources/types
Webhook integration exposes port 3334/webhooks/:id — useful for Zapier, IFTTT, custom apps
acceptanceCriteria: Echo integration generates events every 30s, visible in GET /api/notifications
MCP tool 'service__get_notifications' returns recent events to connected agent
Webhook POST to http://localhost:3334/webhooks/test creates a notification
Notifications are persisted in SQLite (survive daemon restart)
SSE stream at /events delivers real-time notifications to connected clients

title: Dynamic Integration System & Config UI
description: Build the integration management system that allows any integration to be registered dynamically — no code changes required. Implement a terminal UI (TUI) dashboard for operators to add/remove/configure integrations, view live notification feeds, and manage connected MCP servers. Also implement several first-party integration adapters: X/Twitter, Slack, Email (SMTP/IMAP), and a generic HTTP polling integration.

tasks: Create src/cli/dashboard.ts — interactive TUI using blessed or ink: tabbed layout with Live Feed, Integrations, Connected MCPs, Agent Sessions tabs
Create src/cli/integration-wizard.ts — 'service integration add <type>' launches interactive prompts for config fields defined by integration's configSchema
Create src/integrations/builtin/x-twitter.ts — X/Twitter adapter using Twitter API v2: streams mentions/DMs, exposes tools: tweet, reply, retweet, like, search_tweets
Create src/integrations/builtin/slack.ts — Slack adapter using Bolt SDK: listens to messages/mentions, exposes tools: send_message, list_channels, get_thread
Create src/integrations/builtin/email.ts — Email adapter using nodemailer + imap-simple: polls inbox, exposes tools: send_email, list_emails, read_email, reply_email
Create src/integrations/builtin/http-poll.ts — Generic HTTP polling integration: polls any URL on a schedule, creates notification on change/new data
Create src/api/integrations.ts — REST endpoints on port 3334: GET/POST/DELETE /api/integrations, GET /api/integrations/:id/status
Create src/api/mcp-connections.ts — REST endpoints: GET/POST/DELETE /api/mcp-connections (manage downstream MCPs)
Write integration tests for each adapter using mocks
Add 'service integration list', 'service integration add', 'service integration remove' CLI commands
technicalNotes: Integration configs are stored encrypted in SQLite (use built-in crypto for AES-256)
Each integration's configSchema follows JSON Schema Draft 7 — used to drive the wizard prompts
X integration requires TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN, TWITTER_API_KEY/SECRET, ACCESS_TOKEN/SECRET
Slack integration requires SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_APP_TOKEN (Socket Mode)
Email integration requires SMTP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASS + IMAP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASS
TUI dashboard refreshes every 2s, shows last 50 notifications in scrollable list
Connected MCP connections table shows: name, transport, status, tool count, last_seen
acceptanceCriteria: 'service dashboard' opens TUI with Live Feed, Integrations, MCPs, Agents tabs
'service integration add webhook' prompts for config and registers it
X integration (if configured) streams tweets to notification feed
Slack integration (if configured) receives and shows messages
Email integration (if configured) polls and shows new emails
GET /api/integrations returns all configured integrations with status
All integration configs are encrypted at rest in SQLite

title: Agent Context Tools & Action Toolkit
description: Build the full set of MCP tools that AI agents use to interact with SERVICE: reading context/notifications, taking actions across platforms (send tweet, reply email, post Slack message, etc.), managing memory/notes, and subscribing to real-time alerts. This is what makes SERVICE the 'everything tool' — one connection gives the agent full cross-platform capability.

tasks: Create src/tools/notification-tools.ts — MCP tools: get_notifications(filter?, limit?), get_unread_count(), mark_notification_read(id), subscribe_to_events(sources[], types[])
Create src/tools/platform-tools.ts — Dynamic tool generation: iterate all loaded integrations and expose their tools through the MCP hub with integration prefix
Create src/tools/memory-tools.ts — Agent memory: save_note(key, content), get_note(key), list_notes(), delete_note(key) — stored in SQLite
Create src/tools/status-tools.ts — service_status(): returns daemon health, connected integrations, active agent sessions, notification counts
Create src/tools/mcp-management-tools.ts — connect_mcp(name, command, args), disconnect_mcp(name), list_connected_mcps(), get_mcp_tools(name)
Create src/tools/search-tools.ts — search_notifications(query, sources?, dateRange?) — full-text search across notification history
Add 'service__help' tool that returns a formatted guide of all available tools and integrations
Write comprehensive tool tests using MCP test client
Update AGENTS.md and README with full tool documentation
technicalNotes: All tools follow MCP tool schema: name, description, inputSchema (JSON Schema), handler function
Platform tools are generated dynamically when integrations connect/disconnect
Tool result format: { success, data, error?, metadata? }
Memory notes stored in 'agent_notes' SQLite table with agent_id, key, content, updated_at
search_notifications uses SQLite FTS5 for full-text search on notification payloads
Tool names use snake_case, namespaced: service__* for built-ins, integration__tool for platform tools
acceptanceCriteria: AI agent can call 'service__get_notifications' and receive recent events
AI agent can call 'service__service_status' and get health info
AI agent can call 'service__save_note' and 'service__get_note' for cross-session memory
AI agent can call 'service__connect_mcp' to add a new downstream MCP at runtime
Platform tools are visible in MCP tool list when integrations are active
search_notifications returns relevant results with full-text search

title: Polish, Documentation & npm Packaging
description: Final polish pass: comprehensive README with quickstart guide, shell completion, auto-update checker, proper error handling and user-friendly error messages throughout, .env setup guide, example configs for common integrations, and npm package setup so SERVICE can be installed globally with 'npm install -g service-mcp'.

tasks: Write comprehensive README.md: what SERVICE is, architecture diagram (ASCII), quickstart, all CLI commands, integration setup guides, MCP tool reference
Create examples/ directory with example configs for X, Slack, Email, webhook, custom MCP setups
Create docs/integrations/ — per-integration setup guide with screenshots/examples
Add shell completion: 'service completion bash/zsh/fish' generates completion scripts
Add 'service update' command that checks npm for new versions
Audit all error paths — ensure every error has a clear, actionable message for the operator
Add startup banner with version, active integrations count, connected agents count
Create install script: curl-pipe-sh installer that installs globally and runs setup wizard
Set up package.json for npm publish: bin, files, engines fields
Final test run: npm test must pass 100%, coverage report
Create CHANGELOG.md with v1.0.0 entry
Add GitHub Actions workflow for CI (lint, test, build)
technicalNotes: Binary name: 'service' (npm package: 'service-mcp' to avoid conflicts)
Global install: npm install -g service-mcp → exposes 'service' command
Post-install: runs setup wizard if first time
Shell completion using omelette or custom completion generator
README ASCII diagram matches the original SERVICE.png architecture
acceptanceCriteria: npm pack produces a valid package with no missing files
README explains the full system in < 5 minutes reading time
'service --help' shows all commands with descriptions
npm test passes 100% with coverage > 80%
All error paths produce friendly, actionable messages
Example configs are valid and documented
